formats.name: UGC Talking Head
formats.description: Creator speaks directly to camera sharing personal experience with the product.
formats.visual_rules: Single person, phone-quality footage, natural lighting, casual setting (bathroom, bedroom, kitchen). No studio lighting. Text overlay with hook at top.
formats.copy_guidelines: Conversational, first-person. Start with a personal story or hot take. Use contractions and fragments. End with genuine recommendation, not hard sell.

formats.name: UGC Voiceover + B-Roll
formats.description: Voiceover narration over product b-roll, application footage, and lifestyle shots.
formats.visual_rules: Close-up product shots, hands applying product, texture shots, lifestyle b-roll. Fast cuts (1-2s each). Text overlays reinforcing key points.
formats.copy_guidelines: Storytelling arc: problem → discovery → result. Voiceover sounds natural, not scripted. Include specific details (timeframes, before/after).

formats.name: Bold Billboard
formats.description: Large bold headline with minimal supporting text and a product image. High contrast, clean layout.
formats.visual_rules: 1-2 colors max. Headline takes 60%+ of visual space. Product image sharp and prominent. Minimal elements — whitespace is a feature. Sans-serif bold font.
formats.copy_guidelines: Headline: 3-6 words maximum. One supporting line if needed. CTA as button or badge. No paragraphs.

formats.name: Testimonial Card
formats.description: Customer quote as the hero element with star rating, product image, and attribution.
formats.visual_rules: Quote in large text with quotation marks. Star rating (4.5-5 stars). Small product image. Customer first name + detail (age, location). Soft background color.
formats.copy_guidelines: Use exact customer language — don't clean up grammar. Pick quotes with specific outcomes, not generic praise. Attribution adds credibility.

formats.name: Before / After
formats.description: Side-by-side or top-bottom comparison showing transformation.
formats.visual_rules: Split layout: clear before (left/top) and after (right/bottom). Same lighting and angle in both. Labels: 'Before' and 'After' or timeframe ('Day 1' / 'Day 30'). Divider line between panels.
formats.copy_guidelines: Minimal text — the visual does the work. Add timeframe and product name. Optional: one-line testimonial quote below.

formats.name: Listicle / Reasons Why
formats.description: Numbered list of reasons, benefits, or ingredients with supporting visuals.
formats.visual_rules: Numbered items (3-5). Each with icon or small image. Clean alignment. Brand colors for numbers/icons. Product image at bottom or side.
formats.copy_guidelines: Each point: benefit-first, 5-10 words. Lead with the most compelling reason. Last point = CTA-adjacent (e.g., '5. Try it risk-free').

formats.name: Problem → Solution
formats.description: Two-panel layout: top shows the problem (pain), bottom shows the solution (product).
formats.visual_rules: Top panel: muted/desaturated, problem imagery or frustrated expression. Bottom panel: vibrant, product hero shot, satisfied expression. Arrow or transition element between panels.
formats.copy_guidelines: Top: name the pain in audience's language. Bottom: product as the answer. Keep copy short — the contrast does the persuasion.

formats.name: Ingredient Spotlight
formats.description: Single hero ingredient with percentage, benefit, and clinical backing.
formats.visual_rules: Ingredient close-up or macro shot as background. Large percentage or dosage number. Product shown with ingredient highlighted. Clean, clinical aesthetic.
formats.copy_guidelines: Lead with the ingredient and its percentage. One sentence on mechanism (what it does). One line of proof (clinical study, dermatologist endorsement). Avoid jargon — explain in plain language.

formats.name: Educational Carousel
formats.description: Multi-slide educational content that teaches while selling. Each slide builds on the last.
formats.visual_rules: Consistent template across slides. Slide 1 = hook headline. Slides 2-4 = teaching points. Final slide = product CTA. Same colors, fonts, layout per slide. Swipe indicator on slide 1.
formats.copy_guidelines: Slide 1: curiosity hook or myth-busting headline. Middle slides: one point per slide, short text. Final slide: product as the solution to what you just learned.

formats.name: Us vs. Them Comparison
formats.description: Two-column comparison: your product vs. generic/competitor alternative.
formats.visual_rules: Two columns: left = competitor (grey, dull, X marks), right = your brand (brand colors, check marks). Your product elevated, competitor diminished. Clean table or side-by-side format.
formats.copy_guidelines: Compare on 4-6 specific attributes. Use check/X marks. Don't name competitors directly — use 'typical [category]' or 'other brands.' Win on every row.

formats.name: Unboxing / Product Reveal
formats.description: Hands opening packaging, revealing product with ASMR-quality close-ups.
formats.visual_rules: Overhead or 45-degree angle. Clean surface (marble, wood). Slow, deliberate movements. Close-ups of packaging details, textures. Natural lighting preferred.
formats.copy_guidelines: Minimal voiceover — let visuals speak. Text overlays for key product facts. End with product in-use or application shot. Hook: 'Wait for it' or 'The packaging alone...'

formats.name: Founder Story
formats.description: Brand founder speaks about why they created the product — personal, authentic, mission-driven.
formats.visual_rules: Founder on camera, casual but intentional setting (office, lab, kitchen). Mix talking head with product b-roll and behind-the-scenes footage. Lower third with name/title.
formats.copy_guidelines: Personal narrative: I had this problem → nothing worked → I built this. Emotion-driven, not feature-driven. End with invitation, not hard sell.

formats.name: Tutorial / How-To
formats.description: Step-by-step demonstration of how to use the product for best results.
formats.visual_rules: Numbered steps with text overlays. Close-up application shots. Clean, well-lit setting. Timer or progress indicator optional. Before state → application → after state.
formats.copy_guidelines: Step 1, Step 2, Step 3 format. Keep each step to one sentence. Add a pro tip. End with result shot and CTA.

formats.name: Social Proof Mashup
formats.description: Rapid montage of customer testimonials, reviews, results, and reactions.
formats.visual_rules: Fast cuts (1-2s each) of different customers. Mix: selfie videos, text screenshots, before/afters, star ratings. Text overlay count: '10,000+ customers.' Energetic pacing.
formats.copy_guidelines: Minimal scripted copy. Let real customer clips and quotes carry it. Open with volume stat ('Join 50,000+ women who...') or reaction ('Everyone keeps asking what I use').

formats.name: Meme / Native Content
formats.description: Ad disguised as organic meme or relatable content. Blends into feed.
formats.visual_rules: Looks like organic content, not an ad. Meme format, screenshot aesthetic, or text-post style. Minimal branding (small logo if any). Platform-native fonts and spacing.
formats.copy_guidelines: Relatable observation or joke about the audience's pain point. Product mention feels incidental, not forced. Comments-section energy. Self-aware humor OK.

formats.name: Limited-Time Offer
formats.description: Direct response ad with clear offer, discount, and urgency mechanism.
formats.visual_rules: Bold offer text (e.g., '40% OFF'). Countdown or 'ends tonight' badge. Product image with price strikethrough. High contrast CTA button. Urgency colors (red, orange accents).
formats.copy_guidelines: Lead with the offer, not the product. Include original price → sale price. Scarcity element: limited stock, limited time, or exclusive access. Clear CTA: 'Shop Now' or 'Claim Your Discount.'

formats.name: Email Hero Banner
formats.description: Email header image with bold headline and CTA button. Designed for Klaviyo, Mailchimp, or any ESP.
formats.visual_rules: 600px wide (email standard). Bold headline centered. Product image or lifestyle shot as background. CTA button below headline in brand primary color. Keep under 100KB for deliverability.
formats.copy_guidelines: Headline: 5-8 words maximum, matches the email subject line hook. CTA button: 2-3 action words ('Shop the Sale', 'Get Yours'). No body text in the image — that goes in the email copy.

formats.name: Pinterest Pin
formats.description: Vertical lifestyle pin optimized for Pinterest discovery. Aspirational imagery with subtle text overlay.
formats.visual_rules: 1000x1500px (2:3 ratio). Lifestyle imagery dominates — product in context, not on white background. Text overlay: subtle, max 50 characters, positioned top or bottom. Warm, aspirational color palette. Brand logo small and unobtrusive.
formats.copy_guidelines: Text overlay is optional but should be inspirational/aspirational, not salesy. Pin description (separate): 100-200 chars with relevant search keywords. Avoid hashtags — Pinterest uses keyword-based discovery. Speak to aspiration, not pain.

formats.name: TikTok Spark Ad Card
formats.description: Static creative brief card for TikTok Spark Ads — the creative direction document for a creator-led ad.
formats.visual_rules: 9:16 vertical (1080x1920). Designed to look native to TikTok — not polished. Phone-quality aesthetic. Bold text overlays in TikTok-style fonts (white text, black outline). Product shown in-use, not packaged.
formats.copy_guidelines: Hook text: 1 sentence, designed to feel like organic TikTok content. No brand voice — use creator voice. Conversational, imperfect grammar OK. CTA feels like a recommendation ('this is the one'), not a sales pitch.

formats.name: Google Responsive Display Ad
formats.description: Asset set for Google responsive display ads — multiple headlines, descriptions, and image specs.
formats.visual_rules: Landscape image: 1200x628. Square image: 1200x1200. Logo: 1200x1200 (square) and 1200x300 (landscape). All images must work without text overlay (Google may add its own). Clean, uncluttered product shots.
formats.copy_guidelines: Short headlines: 30 chars max (5 variations). Long headline: 90 chars max (1 variation). Descriptions: 90 chars max (5 variations). Business name: 25 chars. Headlines should work independently — Google mixes and matches.
